zh-tw.title: 網路流行語語錄
zh-tw.description: 朋朋、接住、初級大人、I人E人——2026 Threads 上每天在用的流行語，配上情境例句和使用建議。看完不會再被 Z 世代訊息嚇到

en.title: Internet Slang Quotes
en.description: Buddy-buddy, get-caught, junior adult, I-type vs E-type — the slang Taiwanese Threads users actually drop into chat in 2026. Each entry comes with a punchy example line and a usage tip

items.sourceNote: QuickSeek 2026-02 報告：朋朋以 356,929 筆網路聲量居冠

zh-tw.content:
我可以叫你朋朋嗎？
因為男女朋友的「朋」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「朋朋」是 2026 Threads 第一名流行語。比直接叫名字更撩，比叫「寶貝」不油膩。曖昧期想破冰可以直接抄這句
variations: 進階版：「我們先當朋朋，朋的那個朋。」對方會自己腦補完剩下那個字。

en.content:
Can I call you palsy?
Like, the 'pal' in 'partner-pal'.
en.editorNote: The Mandarin original 'pengpeng' is 2026's top Threads slang — uses the first character of 'boyfriend/girlfriend' as a flirty pet name. Use when you want soft flirting without sounding cheesy
variations: Layered version: 'Let's just be palsies for now. The pal in... you know.' Let them fill in the second word themselves.

items.sourceNote: 「接住」2026 Threads 流行語第二名，284,309 筆聲量；自我溫柔語錄頂流動詞

zh-tw.content:
我把今天整個丟給你，
你接得住嗎？

不用解決，
放在你那邊就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「接住」是 2026 自我溫柔語錄頂流動詞，意思是「在崩潰時被人理解」。比說「我累了」具體十倍，傳給願意聽你說話的朋友最有效
variations: 反向版：「今天的你我接住了，你可以先睡。」用來安慰人。

en.content:
I'm tossing my entire day at you.
Think you can catch it?

You don't need to fix anything,
just hold it for a bit.
en.editorNote: 'Catching' someone is the dominant 2026 Taiwan emotional-support verb — it means being held when you're falling apart. Way more specific than 'I'm tired' and reads like real intimacy
variations: Reverse version: 'I caught your today already. Go to sleep.' Use it on a friend who's barely keeping it together.

items.sourceNote: I人/E人 2026 Threads 流行語第三名，266,587 筆聲量；MBTI 縮寫日常化

zh-tw.content:
你是 I 人還是 E 人？

因為我 E 了一整天，
現在只想坐你旁邊充電。
zh-tw.editorNote: I 人／E 人在 2026 已經完全取代「內向／外向」變成日常用語。這句拿來當約會開場白，比問星座潮十倍
variations: 進階版：「我是 I 人偽裝 E 人，你看得出來嗎？」適合認識新朋友破冰。

en.content:
Are you I-type or E-type?

Because I E-ed all day,
and now I just want to recharge sitting next to you.
en.editorNote: I-type / E-type (MBTI shorthand) replaced 'introvert / extrovert' in everyday Taiwan chat in 2026. Way fresher than asking about star signs on a first date
variations: Self-aware version: 'I'm an I-type masquerading as an E-type — can you tell?' Great icebreaker.

items.sourceNote: 2026-02 Threads 爆紅「初級大人」自我檢測格式；dailyview 確認 Q2 viral

zh-tw.content:
面對讚美：
初級大人立刻反駁「沒有沒有我才不是」。
高級大人微笑收下「謝謝你」。

面對挫敗：
初級大人熬夜檢討。
高級大人關燈睡覺。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「初級大人 vs 高級大人」是 2026 Q2 Threads 自我檢測格式。30 歲回家秒變 3 歲，遇到瓦斯保險就喊「大人不在家」，這就是初級大人本人
variations: 工具版：報稅不會就找高級大人，這不是不獨立，是分工。

en.content:
Receiving a compliment:
Junior adult deflects, 'no no, not really.'
Senior adult smiles and says, 'thank you.'

Facing failure:
Junior adult stays up dissecting it.
Senior adult turns off the lights and sleeps.
en.editorNote: 'Junior adult vs senior adult' is the breakout 2026 Q2 self-assessment format on Threads. The original viral moment: a 30-year-old at New Year's Eve dinner saying 'the adults aren't home' when asked to deal with anything official
variations: Practical version: 'When taxes are due, I call a senior adult. This isn't dependence — it's delegation.'

items.sourceNote: 「留友看」蔡英文 3 字留言掀 15 萬讚；2026 Threads 互動工具語

zh-tw.content:
看到好笑的留個「留友看」，
演算法就會把這篇推給你的朋朋。

這年頭最浪漫的事，
是把笑話塞進別人的演算法裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「留友看」是 2026 Threads 工具語，留言這三個字會讓內容更容易出現在朋友動態。前總統蔡英文留這三個字在蕭美琴跳舞影片下，被按 15 萬讚
variations: 懶人版：直接打「留友看」，懶得想留言但又想互動，剛剛好。

en.content:
Saw something funny? Drop a 'leave-it-for-friends' in the replies.
The algorithm will push it to your buddies.

The most romantic thing in 2026:
tucking a joke into someone else's feed.
en.editorNote: 'Leave-it-for-friends' (留友看) is a Taiwan-Threads-specific algorithm hack — comment those three characters and the post is more likely to surface for your friends. Former President Tsai Ing-wen used it under Vice President Hsiao's dance clip and got 150k likes
variations: Lazy version: just type the three words. No clever take needed — that's the whole point.

items.sourceNote: 「硬控」遊戲術語演化；2026 形容「被某人或某物吸引到動彈不得」

zh-tw.content:
你今天的笑
把我硬控了五秒。

我手上的咖啡都涼了，
還是移不開目光。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「硬控」原本是遊戲術語（被技能控制無法行動），2026 變成形容「被某人或某物完全吸引到動彈不得」。誇飾用，配在情話或追星情境效果都好
variations: 追星版：「他直播一開，我就被硬控三小時，連水都忘了喝。」

en.content:
Your smile today
hard-locked me for five seconds.

My coffee went cold in my hand,
and I still couldn't look away.
en.editorNote: 'Hard-lock' (硬控) started as gaming jargon — a stun you can't break out of — and became 2026 slang for 'mesmerized into paralysis.' Works for flirting, fan-girling, anything where you want over-the-top fixation
variations: Stan version: 'His livestream came on and I was hard-locked for three hours. Forgot to drink water.'

items.sourceNote: 「我吃一點」2026-03 台灣 Threads 病毒梗；自嘲共感格式

zh-tw.content:
她問我要不要分她薯條。
我說「我吃一點」。

結果——
我吃了半盒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「我吃一點」是 2026 春天 Threads 病毒梗，反差來自「說好只吃一點」和「實際吃光」之間的距離。傳給每次都被你掃光點心的朋友
variations: 升級版：「最危險的三個字不是『我愛你』，是『我吃一點』。」

en.content:
She offered to share her fries.
I said, 'I'll eat just a little.'

Then —
I ate half the box.
en.editorNote: 'I'll eat a little' (我吃一點) blew up on Taiwan Threads in spring 2026 — the joke is the gap between 'just a taste' and 'cleared the whole thing.' Send to whoever always finishes your snacks
variations: Upgrade: 'The three most dangerous words aren't I love you. They're I'll-eat-a-little.'

items.sourceNote: 「破防」2024 年度流行語延續至 2026；意義為心理防線被打穿

zh-tw.content:
本來假裝沒事。

看到你那句「辛苦了」，
直接破防。

眼淚不講理，
它只認懂你的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「破防」不是玻璃心，是「心理防線真的被打穿」。一句話戳中、一段影片太寫實，整個人瞬間情緒湧上。這句適合在 Threads 分享被理解的瞬間
variations: 反向使用：「我以為我夠堅強，結果一首歌就破防。」

en.content:
I was holding it together —
until you said 'you've worked hard.'

Defenses gone.

Tears don't argue.
They just recognize who actually sees you.
en.editorNote: 'Defenses broken' (破防) doesn't mean fragile — it means a single line or scene punctures the wall you'd built. The 2024 word-of-the-year still going strong in 2026 because emotional honesty is the Threads register
variations: Reverse: 'Thought I was tough. One song in and the defenses were down.'

items.sourceNote: 「情緒價值」2025–2026 高頻關係用語；描述對方提供的安心感

zh-tw.content:
你不用做什麼。
光是陪我發呆，
情緒價值就值九萬八。

我的存款看了，會嫉妒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「情緒價值」是 2026 關係必備詞，描述對方提供的安心感、被理解感。誇張數字會讓這句更有 Threads 節奏，認真說反而會油
variations: 工具版：「他陪你聊天 30 分鐘，情緒價值已經抵過你今天加班三小時。」

en.content:
You don't have to do anything.
Just sitting next to me staring at nothing
is worth a hundred grand in emotional value.

My savings account is jealous.
en.editorNote: 'Emotional value' (情緒價值) is the 2026 must-know relationship term — what someone gives you just by being there and seeing you. Lean into the absurd dollar amount; saying it earnestly reads as cheesy
variations: Practical: 'Thirty minutes with him cancels out your three hours of overtime — that's the conversion rate.'

items.sourceNote: 「活人感」Z 世代 2025–2026 用語；形容看起來真的活著的內容

zh-tw.content:
看到你今天的限動，
有活人感。

我才放心
你還好好的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「活人感」是 Z 世代 2025–2026 用語，形容「看起來真的活著、有生活感」的內容。最近朋友限動很安靜，就傳這句給他，比「你還好嗎」溫柔
variations: 自嘲版：「我今天總算貼了一張有活人感的照片，演算法應該會獎勵我。」

en.content:
Saw the alive-person vibe in your story today.

Okay,
I can stop worrying now.
en.editorNote: 'Alive-person vibe' (活人感) is Gen Z 2025–2026 slang for 'content that proves you're actually living.' If a friend's stories have gone quiet, send this — it's softer than 'are you okay?'
variations: Self-aware: 'I finally posted a story with alive-person vibes today. The algorithm owes me.'

items.sourceNote: MBTI 迷因延伸；2026 取代星座成為日常解讀工具

zh-tw.content:
INFP 朋友傳：「今天好累。」

I 人翻譯：
他這禮拜可能不會出門。

下禮拜也不會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 2026 MBTI 已從心理測驗變成日常迷因。用來翻譯朋友訊息或解釋自己行為，比星座還準。傳給同樣是 I 人的朋友秒懂
variations: 進階版：「ENFP 朋友傳『今天好累』，意思是他要在家辦派對給你看。」

en.content:
INFP friend texts: 'so tired today.'

I-type translation:
he's not leaving the house this week.

Or next week.
en.editorNote: MBTI in 2026 has fully crossed over from personality test to everyday meme — used to translate friends' texts or rationalize your own behavior. Send to a fellow I-type and they'll get it instantly
variations: Advanced: 'ENFP friend texts so tired today — that means they're throwing a party at home and inviting you.'

items.sourceNote: 「好99」誇飾形容詞，2026 IG/Threads 高頻用詞；數字代替程度副詞

zh-tw.content:
我朋友的狗
今天歪頭看了我一眼。

好99，
我已經想包養。

我的銀行帳戶看不懂這句話，
但我懂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「好99」是「好可愛到爆表」的數字誇飾語，2026 IG/Threads 高頻用詞。數字越具體越有節奏，配寵物或反差萌場景無敵
variations: 情境版：「他剛剛說『辛苦了』，好99，我直接戀愛濾鏡開到最大。」

en.content:
My friend's dog
tilted its head at me today.

Way-too-99-cute.
I'm ready to file adoption papers.

My bank account doesn't understand this sentence.
But I do.
en.editorNote: 'So-99' (好99) is 2026 IG/Threads slang where a specific number replaces 'so' for over-the-top cute. The more random the digits, the funnier — works best on pets or surprise softness
variations: Romantic: 'He just said you've worked hard. So-99. The love filter is now at maximum saturation.'

items.sourceNote: 「真冰涼」源自吃播創作者梗；2026 演化為形容人冷淡或事物驚艷的雙義詞

zh-tw.content:
他傳「沒事啊」，
然後三天不讀我訊息。

真冰涼。

冰涼到我以為手機壞了，
結果只是他壞了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「真冰涼」原為食物形容，2026 演化成兩種用法：(1) 驚艷感嘆，(2) 形容人冷淡。這句用第二種，Threads 兩性版高頻吐槽用詞
variations: 驚艷版：「翻到他高中畢業照，真冰涼，原來他十年前就這麼帥。」

en.content:
He texted 'I'm fine,'
then ghosted me for three days.

Truly ice-cold energy.

So cold I thought my phone was broken.
Turns out it was just him.
en.editorNote: 'Truly ice-cold' (真冰涼) started as a food descriptor and split into two 2026 uses: stunned admiration and ghosting commentary. This entry uses the second flavor — peak Taiwan Threads relationship-rant material
variations: Admiring version: 'Found his high school grad photo. Truly ice-cold — he's been this good-looking for ten years.'

items.sourceNote: 「電子布洛芬」2026 Threads 形容能瞬間止痛的療癒內容；醫療詞彙生活化

zh-tw.content:
今天加班到崩潰，
打開朋友傳的貓咪影片——

電子布洛芬。

吃完整個人活過來，
副作用是又能再撐一天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「電子布洛芬」是 2026 Threads 把醫療詞彙生活化的代表，形容看完瞬間止痛的療癒內容。傳給每天靠迷因撐住的朋友最對味
variations: 推薦版：「失戀必備電子布洛芬清單：水獺打架影片、阿嬤跳舞、柴犬走路。」

en.content:
Worked overtime till I cracked.
Opened the cat clip my friend sent —

Digital ibuprofen.

Pain gone in 30 seconds.
Side effect: I can survive one more day.
en.editorNote: 'Digital ibuprofen' (電子布洛芬) is 2026 Taiwan Threads' medical-term-as-everyday-slang for content that kills emotional pain on contact. Send to whoever runs on memes to get through the week
variations: Prescription pad: 'Heartbreak digital-ibuprofen list — otter fights, grandma dancing, shiba inu walking.'

items.sourceNote: 「已讀亂回」2026 Threads 高頻自嘲；形容沒看清訊息就回覆的混亂行為

zh-tw.content:
他問：「你有空嗎？」
我回：「好喔。」

已讀亂回。

等我發現的時候，
我已經答應幫他搬家了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「已讀亂回」是 2026 Threads 自嘲頂流——已讀但根本沒看內容就亂回。比「已讀不回」更有戲劇性，傳給工作分心的朋友秒共感
variations: 升級版：「我不是已讀不回，我是已讀亂回，差別在我至少有試。」

en.content:
He asked: 'Are you free?'
I replied: 'Sure.'

Classic read-and-mis-reply.

By the time I realized,
I'd already agreed to help him move.
en.editorNote: 'Read-and-mis-reply' (已讀亂回) is 2026 Threads' favorite self-roast — you opened the message, didn't actually read it, and answered anyway. More dramatic than 'left on read,' and any distracted desk worker will feel seen
variations: Upgrade: 'I'm not leaving you on read, I'm read-and-mis-replying. The difference is I tried.'

items.sourceNote: 「省電模式」2026 Threads 自我描述用語；I 人/疲勞狀態的科技化形容

zh-tw.content:
今天我開省電模式。

螢幕亮度降到最低，
回訊息只剩單字，
表情包庫存只剩貼圖。

別擔心，
充飽再回你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「省電模式」是 2026 把手機狀態套到自己身上的高頻自我描述。社交電量見底時，預告朋友比硬撐更體貼，I 人收到秒懂
variations: 解釋版：「我不是不理你，我是進入省電模式，背景程式都關了。」

en.content:
I'm on low-power mode today.

Brightness all the way down,
replies trimmed to single words,
emoji reserves running on stickers only.

Don't worry —
I'll text back when I'm charged.
en.editorNote: 'Low-power mode' (省電模式) is 2026 Threads' favorite way to map phone-battery language onto yourself. Announcing it beats white-knuckling through the social drain — fellow I-types will get it instantly
variations: Explainer: 'I'm not ignoring you, I'm in low-power mode. Background apps are all closed.'

items.sourceNote: 「觸爛」源自英文 true 諧音；2026 Threads 強烈認同用語，程度排序：觸 < 觸爛 < 觸爆

zh-tw.content:
朋友：「週末加班的人應該領雙倍薪。」

我：觸爛。

不只觸爛，
是觸爆，
是觸到平流層。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「觸爛」源自英文 true 的諧音，2026 Threads 表強烈認同。程度由弱到強排序：觸 < 觸爛 < 觸爆。比說「對欸」有梗十倍，職場吐槽串底下最常出現
variations: 極致版：「他說週一不該存在，我直接觸爆，現場按愛心三次。」

en.content:
Friend: 'Weekend overtime should pay double.'

Me: True-broken.

Not just true-broken,
it's true-exploded,
it's true-launched-into-the-stratosphere.
en.editorNote: 'True-broken' (觸爛) is a 2026 Taiwan slang from the English 'true' phonetic — used to signal strong agreement. The intensity ladder: true (觸) < true-broken (觸爛) < true-exploded (觸爆). Drop in workplace rant threads to instantly belong
variations: Max version: 'He said Mondays shouldn't exist — I went full true-exploded and triple-tapped the like.'

items.sourceNote: 「M3」源自英文 You know what I'm saying 快速發音；2026 Threads 結尾用語

zh-tw.content:
我不是討厭加班，
我是討厭沒被看見的加班，
M3？

你不用回我，
你點頭我就知道你 M3 了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「M3」源自英文 You know what I'm saying 唸快變成的諧音，2026 Threads 句尾高頻使用，等於「你懂我意思吧」。傳訊息結尾甩一個 M3，比加問號還潮
variations: 簡訊版：「今天不想出門，但又怕躺到下禮拜，M3。」

en.content:
I don't hate overtime —
I hate overtime no one notices,
M3?

You don't have to reply,
just nod and I'll know you M3.
en.editorNote: 'M3' is 2026 Taiwan Threads' favorite sentence-ender — derived from 'you know what I'm saying' said fast enough to sound like M3. Tossing it at the end of a vent reads way fresher than a question mark
variations: Text version: 'Don't want to leave the house, also scared I'll lie here till next week, M3.'

items.sourceNote: 「發瘋文學」2025–2026 跨海華語圈共通梗；用誇張崩潰文體紓壓

zh-tw.content:
我現在打開的不是試算表，
是地獄。

第三欄第七列的數字
在嘲笑我的人生，
我要去廁所跟它對峙——

以上發瘋文學一篇，
寫完繼續上班。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「發瘋文學」是 2025–2026 高頻紓壓文體，用誇張崩潰的句子代替「我好累」。重點是寫完還是要回去上班，這就是當代成年人的療癒儀式
variations: 感情版：「他已讀我四小時，我已經寫完三千字發瘋文學，可以投稿了。」

en.content:
What I just opened isn't a spreadsheet,
it's hell.

The number in column C row 7
is personally mocking my life choices.
I'm going to the bathroom to confront it —

That's one (1) madness-literature post.
Now back to work.
en.editorNote: 'Madness literature' (發瘋文學) is the 2025–2026 cross-Mandarin coping format — over-the-top breakdown prose used in place of 'I'm tired.' The whole bit is that you write it and then go right back to your day job. The ritual is the point
variations: Romance edition: 'He's left me on read for four hours. I've written 3,000 words of madness literature. Ready to submit to a journal.'

items.sourceNote: 「酸民」長青網路用語；2026 Threads 仍高頻使用，反譏酸言酸語的人

zh-tw.content:
酸民留言：「也沒多好看。」

我回：謝謝你今天的酸度。

沒有你，
我的留言區會少一份檸檬，
也會少一份免費的演算法流量。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「酸民」指網路上專門酸言酸語的人，2026 Threads 仍是高頻用語。比起對嗆，把對方當演算法燃料更高級，這句適合貼文被無聊人嘴的時候
variations: 佛系版：「謝謝酸民，我不討厭你，演算法討厭空白留言。」

en.content:
Sour-citizen comment: 'It's not that good.'

Me: thanks for today's acidity.

Without you,
my comment section would be short one lemon,
and short one free boost from the algorithm.
en.editorNote: 'Sour citizens' (酸民) is the long-running Taiwan term for trolls who specialize in petty negativity, still going strong on 2026 Threads. The high-class move isn't clapping back — it's framing them as algorithm fuel. Pull this out when a stranger nitpicks your post
variations: Zen version: 'Thanks, sour citizen — I don't hate you. The algorithm hates empty comment sections.'

items.sourceNote: 「各各」2026 年輕人用語；表達掃興、被打斷興致的情緒反應

zh-tw.content:
我精心化好妝準備出門，
他傳來：「我們改約下週好嗎？」

各各。

睫毛膏的努力
不會原諒這種事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「各各」是 2026 年輕世代表達「整個興致被澆熄」的單詞，比「掃興」更短更有節奏感。臨時被放鳥、計劃被打亂的時候用最對味
variations: 簡訊版：「下雨了，各各，今天的露營改成在家追劇。」

en.content:
Spent an hour on my makeup, ready to head out.
He texts: 'can we reschedule for next week?'

Mood: extinguished.

The mascara's labor
will not forgive this.
en.editorNote: 'Mood extinguished' (各各) is 2026 Taiwan Gen Z's one-word reaction for when your hype gets snuffed out. Shorter and snappier than 'buzzkill' — pull it out when plans get cancelled at the last second
variations: Text version: 'It's raining. Mood extinguished. Today's camping trip is now a Netflix marathon.'

items.sourceNote: 「暈爛」2025–2026 高頻戀愛用語；形容被某人迷得徹底神魂顛倒

zh-tw.content:
他只是把外套披在我身上，
沒說話。

我暈爛。

暈到走路都在轉圈，
暈到回家還在傻笑。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「暈爛」是 2026 Threads 戀愛版頂流——比「心動」誇張十倍，形容對某人的好感已經爆表到失去判斷力。寫追星貼文或閨密曖昧分享都好用
variations: 追星版：「他鏡頭一轉看向我，我直接暈爛三天，工作都不想做。」

en.content:
He just draped his jacket over my shoulders.
No words.

I'm dizzy-wrecked.

Dizzy enough to walk in circles,
dizzy enough to grin all the way home.
en.editorNote: 'Dizzy-wrecked' (暈爛) is 2026 Taiwan Threads' top-tier crush vocabulary — way more dramatic than 'butterflies,' it means you've lost all judgment to one person. Perfect for stan posts or telling your group chat about a new flirt
variations: Stan version: 'Camera panned to him, I was dizzy-wrecked for three days. Couldn't even open my laptop.'

items.sourceNote: 「超頂」2025–2026 高頻讚嘆用語；形容某事物頂到不能再頂

zh-tw.content:
今天的便當
是阿姨多塞了一塊雞腿的便當。

超頂。

沒有什麼比
被陌生人偷偷加菜更頂的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「超頂」是 2026 高頻讚嘆詞，意思是「頂到天花板的好」。比「超讚」更有畫面，配在意外驚喜或被照顧的瞬間最對味
variations: 誇人版：「他默默幫我留了最後一塊蛋糕，超頂，這就是會做人。」

en.content:
Today's lunchbox
came with a bonus drumstick the auntie snuck in.

Peak energy.

Nothing tops
a stranger quietly upgrading your day.
en.editorNote: 'Peak energy' (超頂) is 2026 Taiwan slang for 'good all the way to the ceiling.' More vivid than 'amazing' — drop it when something tiny but thoughtful made your whole day
variations: Praising version: 'He quietly saved me the last slice of cake. Peak energy. That's how you do it.'

items.sourceNote: 「從從容容游刃有餘」2025 政治金句延燒 2026；用反差製造幽默

zh-tw.content:
別人上班：
從從容容，游刃有餘。

我上班：
匆匆忙忙，連滾帶爬。

同樣是九點打卡，
命運差很大。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「從從容容游刃有餘 vs 匆匆忙忙連滾帶爬」是 2025 政治金句變身的 2026 反差迷因。對比自己跟「人生勝利組」的日常，越誇張越好笑
variations: 戀愛版：「別人談戀愛從從容容游刃有餘，我談戀愛匆匆忙忙連滾帶爬。」

en.content:
Other people at work:
cool, calm, totally in control.

Me at work:
rushing, scrambling, basically rolling in.

Same 9am clock-in,
wildly different fates.
en.editorNote: 'Cool-and-collected vs scrambling-and-tumbling' is the 2026 contrast meme born from a 2025 Taiwan political quote. Pair your messy reality against someone living their best life — the more exaggerated, the better
variations: Dating version: 'Other people date cool and collected. I date scrambling and tumbling in.'

items.sourceNote: 「很躁」2026 Threads 高頻情緒詞；形容煩躁無處發洩的瞬間

zh-tw.content:
冷氣壞了，
網路斷了，
咖啡灑了，
群組還在 tag 我。

我很躁。

躁到想把自己
切到飛航模式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「很躁」是 2026 Threads 形容「煩到失控但又只能忍著」的高頻詞。比「煩」更立體，連續發生小事的時候用最有共感
variations: 上班版：「會議改第三次時間，我很躁，但只能微笑回 OK。」

en.content:
AC broken,
Wi-Fi down,
coffee spilled,
and the group chat keeps tagging me.

I'm fried-and-restless.

Fried enough to want to flip
my whole self to airplane mode.
en.editorNote: 'Fried-and-restless' (很躁) is 2026 Taiwan Threads' favorite word for 'irritated past my limit but stuck holding it in.' More textured than 'annoyed' — use it when small things keep stacking
variations: Office version: 'They rescheduled the meeting three times. I'm fried-and-restless, but I still smile-typed an OK.'

items.sourceNote: 「泉」源自台語「濺」（tsuānn）；2026 Threads 吐槽吹牛專用詞

zh-tw.content:
他說他這個月要存十萬。

泉。

上禮拜的珍奶錢
還是我幫他付的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「泉」源自台語「濺」（tsuānn），意思是「你不要唬爛了」。2026 Threads 上專門用來吐槽朋友吹牛，比直接嗆「騙人」有戲多了
variations: 回應版：「他說他要早睡，我直接回一個泉，連解釋都懶。」

en.content:
He says he's saving ten grand this month.

Cap.

Last week's bubble tea money
still came out of my wallet.
en.editorNote: 'Cap' here translates 泉 (from Taiwanese 'tsuānn,' meaning to spout nonsense) — 2026 Taiwan Threads' go-to clapback when a friend is obviously bluffing. Snappier than 'liar,' funnier than silence
variations: Reply version: 'He said he's going to bed early. I just sent back cap. Didn't even need to explain.'

items.sourceNote: 「嘻嘻不嘻嘻」2026 Threads 情緒翻轉迷因；形容心情瞬間掉到冰點

zh-tw.content:
領到薪水，嘻嘻。
打開帳單，不嘻嘻。

錢進來三秒，
出去三萬，
嘻嘻不嘻嘻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「嘻嘻不嘻嘻」是 2026 Threads 情緒翻轉迷因，描述心情從天堂掉到地獄的瞬間。配在「期待 vs 現實」的反差場景無敵共感
variations: 戀愛版：「他傳早安，嘻嘻。後面那句是傳錯人，不嘻嘻。」

en.content:
Payday hits — yay.
Open the bills — not yay.

Money landed for three seconds,
thirty grand walked out,
yay-not-yay.
en.editorNote: 'Yay-not-yay' (嘻嘻不嘻嘻) is 2026 Taiwan Threads' favorite mood-flip meme — capturing the second your feelings nosedive from happy to crushed. Perfect for any 'expectation vs. reality' pairing
variations: Dating version: 'He sent good morning — yay. Turns out it was meant for someone else — not yay.'

items.sourceNote: 「好解」2025–2026 Threads 共感用語；表達深度理解或同理

zh-tw.content:
她說：「明明很累，
但躺到床上又睡不著。」

好解。

身體在罷工，
腦袋還在加班，
這就是當代人的標配。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「好解」是 2026 Threads 高頻共感用詞，比「我懂」更有重量，意思是「我完全理解到不用解釋」。傳給講出你心聲的朋友最適合
variations: 回覆版：「他說週末比上班還累，我只回兩個字：好解。」

en.content:
She said: 'I'm exhausted,
but the second I lie down I can't sleep.'

Felt that.

Body's on strike,
brain's still pulling overtime —
the standard modern issue starter pack.
en.editorNote: 'Felt that' translates 好解 — 2026 Taiwan Threads' favorite empathy reply, heavier than 'same' because it implies 'I get it without you needing to explain.' Send when a friend nails exactly what you've been feeling
variations: Reply version: 'He said weekends feel more tiring than work. I just sent back: felt that.'

items.sourceNote: 「這我」2025–2026 Threads 自我代入用語；省略「就是」直接認領

zh-tw.content:
迷因：星期一早上的人類
是一坨會呼吸的棉被。

這我。

沒有要解釋，
也不用為自己辯護，
直接認領。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「這我」是 2026 Threads 迷因留言區頂流，省略「這就是我」直接兩個字認領自己。被迷因戳中本人時最常用
variations: 工具版：「圖文：下班後只想躺著滑手機。這我，連回應都懶。」

en.content:
Meme: Monday morning humans
are a breathing pile of duvet.

That's me.

No explanation,
no self-defense,
just full custody claimed.
en.editorNote: 'That's me' (這我) is the 2026 Threads meme-section champion — strips 'that is literally me' down to two characters and just claims it. Use whenever a meme calls you out by name
variations: Practical: 'Caption: after work I only want to lie down and scroll. That's me. Too tired to even react.'

items.sourceNote: 「句點王」2026 Threads 對話無解者代名詞；形容讓對話直接死掉的人

zh-tw.content:
我：「今天天氣不錯欸。」
他：「嗯。」

句點王本人。

我準備了三個話題，
他用一個字
全部送進垃圾桶。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「句點王」是 2026 Threads 形容「讓對話瞬間斷掉的人」。曖昧期被句點最痛，貼這句到限動，懂的人都懂
variations: 自嘲版：「我才是句點王，他傳一段話我只回貼圖。」

en.content:
Me: 'Nice weather today.'
Him: 'Mm.'

Certified conversation killer.

I lined up three topics —
he used one syllable
to delete all of them.
en.editorNote: 'Conversation killer' (句點王, literally 'King of Periods') is 2026 Taiwan Threads' label for someone whose replies instantly murder a chat. Most painful during a flirt phase — drop this on your story and the right people will nod
variations: Self-roast: 'I'm the real conversation killer — he sends a paragraph, I reply with a sticker.'

items.sourceNote: 「咩噗」源自周興哲歌詞「每一步」諧音；2026 Threads 撒嬌哭哭用語

zh-tw.content:
今天的便當
阿姨忘了幫我加滷蛋。

咩噗。

沒有滷蛋的便當，
就像沒有你的週末，
空空的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「咩噗」源自周興哲歌詞「每一步」的諧音，2026 Threads 撒嬌型哭哭用語。比直接打「哭哭」更可愛，配在小事委屈的場景最甜
variations: 回應版：「他傳「我先睡了」，咩噗，我故事還沒講完。」

en.content:
Today's lunchbox
came without the braised egg.

Miepu.

A lunchbox with no egg
is like a weekend without you —
just... empty.
en.editorNote: 'Miepu' (咩噗) is a 2026 Taiwan Threads soft-cry word — born from the lyric '每一步' in a Chou Hsing-che song that sounds like miepu. Sweeter than typing 'sad face,' best paired with tiny everyday letdowns
variations: Reply version: 'He texted I'm going to sleep. Miepu. I wasn't done with my story.'

items.sourceNote: 「超派」源自 Toyz 與超哥事件；2026 演化為形容凶悍、強勢、霸氣的形容詞

zh-tw.content:
她直接走進會議室，
把那份報告甩在桌上：
「重做。」

超派。

我那一瞬間
決定這輩子追隨她。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「超派」原本是社會事件衍生詞，2026 已經洗白成形容「霸氣、強勢到讓人服氣」。誇女主管、追星本命、硬派行為都好用，比「帥」更立體
variations: 追星版：「她舞台一站定，鏡頭一掃，超派，我直接被收編。」

en.content:
She walked straight into the meeting room,
slapped the report onto the table:
'Do it again.'

Full boss-mode.

That was the moment
I decided I'd follow her anywhere.
en.editorNote: 'Boss-mode' (超派) started as a viral incident phrase and reformed in 2026 into a compliment for 'so commanding you just submit.' Use on female bosses, your bias, anyone with unapologetic presence — way more textured than 'cool'
variations: Stan version: 'She hit the stage mark, camera swept across — full boss-mode. I was instantly recruited.'

items.sourceNote: 「偷感」2025–2026 跨海華語圈用語；形容低調做事不想被注意的氣場

zh-tw.content:
我去公司茶水間倒水，
走路像在偵探片裡。

偷感很重。

不是我做了什麼，
是我光呼吸
就怕打擾到別人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「偷感」2026 跨海華語圈共通用語，形容「明明沒做壞事，但動作小心翼翼像在偷東西」的氣場。I 人日常代名詞，自嘲超對味
variations: 進階版：「她講話偷感很重，每句後面都默默加一個『不好意思』。」

en.content:
Walked to the office pantry to fill my bottle.
Moved like I was in a heist film.

Maximum sneaky-vibe.

It's not that I did anything wrong —
I'm just breathing
like I'm afraid of disturbing someone.
en.editorNote: 'Sneaky-vibe' (偷感) is the 2026 cross-Mandarin term for 'I'm not doing anything wrong, but I move like a thief anyway.' The unofficial I-type starter pack — leans into self-deprecation perfectly
variations: Advanced: 'She talks with so much sneaky-vibe, every sentence quietly ends in sorry.'

items.sourceNote: 「主角能量」源自 Gen Z「main character energy」翻譯；2026 Threads 自我打氣語

zh-tw.content:
今天起床
我決定開主角能量。

捷運走道是紅毯，
通勤包是奧斯卡獎座，
咖啡灑在襯衫上
是劇本裡的鋪陳。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「主角能量」是 Gen Z「main character energy」翻成中文後的 2026 版本，意思是「把自己當人生主角過日子」。低潮時拿來幫自己打氣，比正能量語錄不油膩
variations: 工具版：「下班開主角能量，配一首主題曲走出辦公室，加班也值得。」

en.content:
Woke up today
and decided to switch on main-character energy.

The MRT aisle is a red carpet,
my tote is an Oscar statuette,
the coffee on my shirt
is just foreshadowing.
en.editorNote: 'Main-character energy' got localized into Mandarin Threads in 2026 as the go-to self-pep phrase — 'live your life like you're the lead.' Less cheesy than a motivational quote, perfect when you need to romanticize a Tuesday
variations: Practical: 'Clocked out, switched on main-character energy, queued my theme song — even overtime felt cinematic.'

items.sourceNote: 「人類圖鑑」2026 Threads 把日常人觀察整理成圖鑑式格式；自帶幽默距離感

zh-tw.content:
今日人類圖鑑：

捷運上一邊跑一邊吃飯糰的上班族。
生態：邊咀嚼邊滑手機，動作精準。
出沒地：早上 8 點 47 分的轉乘站。
備註：飯糰永遠不會掉，這是天賦。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「人類圖鑑」是 2026 Threads 把日常觀察整理成圖鑑格式的迷因玩法。自帶幽默距離感，比直接吐槽更高級，貼出來會被各種人對號入座
variations: 情境版：「人類圖鑑：開會永遠最後到的同事。出沒地：第十分鐘的玻璃門。」

en.content:
Today's human field guide:

The office worker eating a rice ball mid-sprint on the MRT.
Behavior: chews and scrolls simultaneously, surgical precision.
Habitat: the transfer station at 8:47 a.m.
Note: the rice ball never falls. It's a gift.
en.editorNote: 'Human field guide' (人類圖鑑) is the 2026 Threads format that turns everyday people-watching into mock zoological entries. Reads as observational comedy — funnier than direct mocking, and someone always claims they're the entry
variations: Scenario: 'Human field guide: the coworker who's always last to the meeting. Habitat: the glass door at minute ten.'

items.sourceNote: 「冷靜了」2026 跨海華語圈用語，從食物放涼演化為「酷酷不在乎」生活態度

zh-tw.content:
他傳「在忙」三天才回我。

好喔，
那我也冷靜了。

手機放抽屜，
感情也放抽屜，
一起涼著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「冷靜了」原本是把熱湯放涼的動作，2026 演化成「我不再為這件事情上頭」的酷態度。被晾的時候用最痛快，比生氣有風度十倍
variations: 工具版：「老闆說急件改下週，我直接冷靜了，鍵盤都開飛航。」

en.content:
He took three days to reply 'busy.'

Cool,
I'm chilled now too.

Phone in the drawer,
feelings in the drawer,
letting it all go cold together.
en.editorNote: 'Chilled now' (冷靜了) originally meant letting hot soup cool, but in 2026 it became the slang for 'I'm officially over this.' Drop it when someone leaves you hanging — way classier than rage-typing
variations: Workplace version: 'Boss bumped my urgent task to next week — I went chilled now, keyboard on airplane mode.'

items.sourceNote: 「也太」2026 Threads 高頻誇飾起手式；後接形容詞放大反差感

zh-tw.content:
他默默把我的雨傘
放在我桌上就走了。

也太。

沒說一句話，
但整個下午我心裡都在下小雨。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「也太」是 2026 Threads 留言區頂流起手式，故意停在那邊不接完的省略法，後面接什麼都行（也太可愛、也太扯、也太好哭）。比把整句講完更有情緒
variations: 驚嚇版：「她直接把報告丟我臉上，也太，我連反駁都來不及。」

en.content:
He left my umbrella
quietly on my desk and walked off.

This is too —

Didn't say a word,
but my chest's been drizzling all afternoon.
en.editorNote: 'This is too —' (也太) is 2026 Threads' favorite cliffhanger opener. You start the sentence and just... don't finish it (too cute, too wild, too sad). The unfinished beat hits harder than the full reaction
variations: Shocked version: 'She slammed the report on my face — this is too —, I didn't even get to argue back.'

items.sourceNote: 「上頭」2025–2026 Threads 高頻動詞；形容對某人事物失去理智沉迷

zh-tw.content:
我只是點開那部劇的預告，
結果——

上頭。

一路追到清晨四點，
洗衣機還在等我，
我先等男主開口。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「上頭」是 2026 Threads 形容「失去理智沉迷某件事」的高頻動詞。比「迷上」誇張，比「中毒」生活化，追劇、追星、追新口味雞排都好用
variations: 戀愛版：「我以為只是聊得來，沒想到上頭，連他傳貼圖我都能解讀三層意思。」

en.content:
I just clicked the trailer.
Then —

Fully head-gone.

Binged till 4 a.m.,
the laundry's still waiting,
I'm waiting for the lead to speak first.
en.editorNote: 'Head-gone' (上頭) is 2026 Taiwan Threads' favorite verb for 'lost all reason to this thing.' More dramatic than 'hooked,' more everyday than 'addicted' — works for binge-watching, stanning, or chasing a new fried chicken flavor
variations: Dating version: 'Thought we just clicked. Turns out I'm head-gone — I'm decoding his stickers in three layers now.'

items.sourceNote: 「整個人鬆掉」2026 Threads 解壓格式；形容緊繃後突然放鬆的瞬間

zh-tw.content:
週五下班的捷運，
看到夕陽正好打進車廂——

整個人鬆掉。

這禮拜的我，
總算把肩膀
從耳朵旁邊拿下來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「整個人鬆掉」是 2026 Threads 描述「緊繃太久突然放鬆」的高頻片語。很有畫面，比「終於放假」具體十倍，配週末或下班場景最對味
variations: 工具版：「會議終於取消，整個人鬆掉，我決定先趴桌十分鐘。」

en.content:
Friday MRT, heading home.
Sunset hits the car at the right angle —

Entire body unclenched.

This week's me
finally pulled my shoulders
down from beside my ears.
en.editorNote: 'Entire body unclenched' (整個人鬆掉) is 2026 Taiwan Threads' favorite phrase for the moment a long stretch of tension breaks. Way more visual than 'finally relaxed' — pair with a Friday clock-out or unexpectedly cancelled meeting
variations: Office version: 'Meeting got cancelled — entire body unclenched. Decided to faceplant on my desk for ten minutes.'

items.sourceNote: 「尊嘟假嘟」2025–2026 Threads 高頻疑問詞；可愛化的「真的假的」

zh-tw.content:
他說他不喝手搖一個月了。

尊嘟假嘟？

我認識的他
上禮拜還在排第二杯半價。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「尊嘟假嘟」是「真的假的」的可愛諧音版，2026 Threads 表達驚訝跟懷疑都好用。比直接打問號多一份戲，閨密群組吐槽必備
variations: 簡訊版：「老闆說今天不開會，尊嘟假嘟，我先開心十分鐘。」

en.content:
He says he's been off bubble tea for a whole month.

For real for real?

The him I know
was in the buy-one-get-one line last week.
en.editorNote: 'For real for real' translates 尊嘟假嘟, the cutesy phonetic spin on 'is that true or fake' that's owned 2025–2026 Taiwan Threads. Carries more drama than a plain question mark — group-chat-of-best-friends approved
variations: Text version: 'Boss said no meetings today — for real for real? Letting myself be happy for ten minutes.'

items.sourceNote: 「來都來了」2025–2026 自我說服迷因；用一句話合理化任何衝動消費

zh-tw.content:
我只是進去逛逛。

來都來了，
試穿一下。

來都來了，
刷下去。

離開時手上多了三袋，
錢包少了三千。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「來都來了」是 2026 自我說服頂流金句，從旅遊景點蔓延到逛街、聚餐、追劇。一句話可以合理化任何衝動，傳給每次都被你拖下水的朋友剛剛好
variations: 聚餐版：「都九點了還要吃宵夜？來都來了，加一份雞排。」

en.content:
I just went in to browse.

Well, since I'm already here —
tried it on.

Since I'm already here —
swiped the card.

Walked out with three bags,
minus three grand from the wallet.
en.editorNote: 'Since I'm already here' (來都來了) is 2026 Taiwan's reigning self-justification phrase — leaped from tourist traps to shopping, dinners, and binge sessions. One line legalizes any impulse. Send to whoever you keep dragging into trouble
variations: Dinner version: 'It's already 9pm and we want late-night food? Since we're already here — add a fried chicken.'

items.sourceNote: 「氣氛組」2025–2026 Threads 自嘲用語；形容只負責活絡氣氛、不出實力的角色

zh-tw.content:
聚餐時負責點菜的是高級大人，
負責結帳的是冤大頭，
我？

氣氛組組長。

專長是笑得最大聲，
吃得最快，
結帳時剛好去廁所。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「氣氛組」是 2026 Threads 自嘲用語，形容「不出實力只活絡氣氛」的角色。比說「我廢」幽默，朋友聚餐被點名做事時甩這句保平安
variations: 工作版：「會議裡我是氣氛組，老闆講笑話我負責笑最大聲。」

en.content:
At dinner, the senior adult orders.
The sucker pays.
Me?

Vibes-team captain.

Specialty: loudest laugh,
fastest chewer,
conveniently in the bathroom when the bill arrives.
en.editorNote: 'Vibes team' (氣氛組) is 2026 Taiwan Threads' self-roast for 'I bring no skills, only atmosphere.' Funnier than calling yourself useless — pull it out when friends try to assign you a real task at dinner
variations: Work version: 'In meetings I'm vibes team — when the boss tells a joke I provide the loudest laugh.'

items.sourceNote: 「蛤」2026 Threads 不滿/驚訝/賣萌多用單字；語氣全靠拉長音

zh-tw.content:
他說：「下週要開會，麻煩你做簡報。」

短促的「蛤？」是驚嚇。
拉長的「蛤——」是不滿。
軟綿綿的「蛤～」是撒嬌想偷懶。

同一個字，三種人生。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「蛤」是 2026 Threads 萬用語氣詞，全靠拉長音表達不同情緒。短促＝驚訝，拉長＝不滿，軟綿＝撒嬌。職場、感情、家人群組都適用
variations: 情境版：「媽傳『今天加菜』，我回『蛤～』，意思是我馬上回家。」

en.content:
He says: 'There's a meeting next week, can you handle the slides?'

A sharp 'huh?' = shock.
A dragged-out 'huuuuh —' = displeasure.
A soft 'huuh~' = flirty bargaining.

One syllable, three lives.
en.editorNote: 'Huh' (蛤) is 2026 Taiwan Threads' Swiss-army interjection — the meaning is entirely in how long you stretch the vowel. Short = shock, long = sulk, soft = wheedle. Works in offices, relationships, and family group chats alike
variations: Scenario: 'Mom texts extra dishes tonight — I send back huuh~. That means I'm already heading home.'

items.sourceNote: 「觸爛」2026 Threads 強烈贊同用語；觸 < 觸爛 < 觸爆，「觸」為英文 true 諧音

zh-tw.content:
朋友抱怨：「禮拜一最廢，禮拜五最神。」

我：「觸。」
他再說：「但禮拜五晚上還是要排隊吃飯。」
我：「觸爛。」
他補刀：「結果一個位子都沒有。」
我：「觸爆。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「觸」是英文 true 的諧音，2026 Threads 用來表達認同；強度可疊加：觸 < 觸爛 < 觸爆。比「對啊」生動，比「+1」有溫度，朋友吐苦水時甩這組三連發最對味
variations: 單刀版：對方講出真理就回一個字「觸。」，比五百字長文更殺。

en.content:
Friend rants: 'Mondays are trash, Fridays are god-tier.'

Me: 'Truu.'
Friend: 'But Friday nights still mean queueing for dinner.'
Me: 'Truu-to-the-bone.'
Friend: 'And no tables left.'
Me: 'Truu-pocalypse.'
en.editorNote: 'Truu' (觸) is the 2026 Taiwan Threads agreement noise — Mandarin homophone of English 'true.' Stack it for intensity: truu < truu-to-the-bone < truu-pocalypse. Warmer than '+1,' punchier than 'agreed.' Pull the triple combo when a friend is venting
variations: One-word kill: when someone drops absolute truth, just reply 'Truu.' Hits harder than a five-hundred-word essay.

items.sourceNote: 「M3」2026 Z 世代用語；英文 you know what I'm saying 念快變 nam saying，再縮成 M3

zh-tw.content:
我跟你說，
薪水沒漲，物價漲，
房租漲，咖啡也漲，
只有我的存款很穩定地不漲。

M3？
zh-tw.editorNote: 「M3」是英文「you know what I'm saying」念快變「nam saying」再縮寫成的 2026 Z 世代口頭禪。講完一段感受丟一個 M3，比「你懂吧」潮十倍。注意：要用在熟人之間，職場群組慎用
variations: 短評版：「今天累爛，M3？」一句帶過所有細節。

en.content:
Like I'm telling you,
salary flat, prices up,
rent up, coffee up,
only my savings stay impressively stable.

M3?
en.editorNote: 'M3' is 2026 Taiwan Z-slang — short for 'nam saying,' itself a fast-spoken collapse of 'you know what I'm saying.' Drop it after a vent and it lands cooler than 'you feel me?' Best kept for friends — don't fire it in a work group chat
variations: Quick-hit: 'Wrecked today, M3?' One line replaces the whole story.

items.sourceNote: 「很躁」2025–2026 持續發酵的網路情緒詞；指煩、煩到坐不住、煩到想罵人

zh-tw.content:
冷氣壞了，很躁。
捷運誤點，很躁。
群組訊息一直跳，很躁。
好不容易躺下來，
蚊子在耳邊開演唱會。

躁爛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「很躁」是 2026 Threads 仍然頂流的情緒詞，比「煩」更具體，帶身體感──煩到坐不住、想罵髒話又不想動。事情累積到頂點時用「躁爛」收尾最爽
variations: 簡訊版：朋友問「在幹嘛」，回「躁。」三個字勝過抱怨段落。

en.content:
AC broken — antsy.
MRT delayed — antsy.
Group chat won't stop pinging — antsy.
Finally lie down,
and a mosquito starts a concert in my ear.

Maxed-out antsy.
en.editorNote: 'Antsy' (很躁) is the 2026 Taiwan Threads emotion-word still topping the charts — more visceral than 'annoyed,' a body-level itch to swear without the energy to move. When everything stacks, finish with 'maxed-out antsy' (躁爛)
variations: Text version: friend asks 'wyd?' — just reply 'Antsy.' Beats a whole paragraph of complaint.

items.sourceNote: 「超頂」2026 Threads 持續使用；形容人事物表現極佳、頂尖到令人服氣

zh-tw.content:
朋友自己烤了一條吐司，
切片、抹奶油、撒糖粉，
還配自製手沖咖啡。

我喝了一口：
「超頂。」

他笑得很驕傲，
但下一秒問我借錢買咖啡豆。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「超頂」是 2026 Threads 萬用稱讚語，形容某人某事頂尖到讓人甘拜下風。比「很厲害」短、比「神」有重量，吃到、看到、收到神等級的東西就甩這兩個字
variations: 反差版：「他唱歌超頂，講話超不行。」用來描述同一個人優缺點兩極化。

en.content:
Friend bakes their own loaf,
slices it, butters it, dusts powdered sugar,
pairs it with hand-poured coffee.

First sip:
'Peak.'

They grin, proud —
then ask to borrow money for more beans.
en.editorNote: 'Peak' (超頂) is the 2026 Taiwan Threads catch-all compliment — top-tier, you-win, hands-down. Shorter than 'amazing,' weightier than 'cool.' Use when food, art, or a friend's effort genuinely beats your expectations
variations: Contrast version: 'His singing is peak, his small talk is the opposite.' Great for describing someone with split-personality strengths.

items.sourceNote: 「高級大人」2026 Threads 同名梗下半段；指真的把生活過得井然有序、可以照顧別人的成年人

zh-tw.content:
高級大人的早晨：
六點起床、自己做早餐、回完所有訊息。

我的早晨：
鬧鐘響八次、抓一條昨天的褲子、邊刷牙邊罵天氣。

果然還在初級。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「高級大人」是 2026 Threads「初級大人／高級大人」自我檢測梗的另一半，形容那種真的把生活過好、還有餘力照顧別人的成年人。要拉開反差感，先寫高級大人版本，再寫自己的真實版本最有效
variations: 稱讚版：「我朋友三十歲就買房養貓還有副業，根本高級大人本人。」用來把朋友抬到神壇上。

en.content:
Senior adult morning:
up at 6, homemade breakfast, all texts replied to.

My morning:
alarm snoozed 8 times, grab yesterday's pants, brush teeth while cursing the weather.

Still firmly junior.
en.editorNote: 'Senior adult' (高級大人) is the upper half of the 2026 Taiwan Threads junior/senior adult meme — someone who has actually got their life together with energy to spare for others. The trick: write the idealized version first, then your honest version, and let the gap do the comedy
variations: Compliment version: 'My friend bought a place at 30, has a cat AND a side hustle — full senior adult.' Use it to put a friend on a pedestal.

items.sourceNote: 「已購買小孩愛吃」2025 中年家長迷因 → 2026 Threads 嘲諷式延伸用法

zh-tw.content:
我看 IG 廣告。
商品：醜得像被踩過的吐司。

滑下去看評價，
第一則：「已購買小孩愛吃。」

好，我下單了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「已購買小孩愛吃」原本是中年家長在電商評論區的萬用留言，2026 變成 Threads 上的嘲諷式句尾——用來自嘲被家長型評論說服，或形容自己被低門檻訊息打中的瞬間
variations: 錯位版：朋友推薦冷門 indie 樂團，回「已下載小孩愛聽。」用反差製造笑點。

en.content:
Scrolling IG ads.
Product: looks like toast someone stepped on.

I scroll to reviews.
First one: 'Bought it. Kids love it.'

Fine. Adding to cart.
en.editorNote: 'Bought it, kids love it' (已購買小孩愛吃) started as the universal mom-and-dad e-commerce review and became a 2026 Threads punchline — used to roast yourself for caving to low-effort persuasion, or anytime a basic message lands embarrassingly hard
variations: Off-key version: friend recommends an obscure indie band — reply 'Downloaded. Kids love it.' The mismatch is the joke.

items.sourceNote: 「誰敢想」2025–2026 持續流行的爆紅迷因；用於人生成就、誇張對比的開場

zh-tw.content:
二十歲：以為三十歲會有房、有狗、有對象。
三十歲：有房租、有貓毛過敏、有 LINE 群組三百個。

誰敢想。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「誰敢想」是 2025–2026 在 Threads 還活著的感慨式收尾，原意是「誰敢想到會變成這樣」。當下與當年的反差越大越好笑，最後丟「誰敢想」三字就完工
variations: 互動版：朋友傳近況，回「誰敢想啊。」一句帶過所有起伏，省下五百字。

en.content:
At 20: figured 30 would mean a house, a dog, a partner.
At 30: rent, cat-fur allergy, three hundred LINE group chats.

Who would've thought.
en.editorNote: 'Who would've thought' (誰敢想) is the 2025–2026 Taiwan Threads sigh-closer — literally 'who'd dare to imagine.' The bigger the gap between then and now, the funnier. Just stack the contrast and drop the three words at the end
variations: Reply version: friend shares life update — answer 'Who would've thought.' One line covers every ups-and-downs, saves you 500 words.

items.sourceNote: 「留友看」2025-2026 Threads 互動演算法觸發詞；觀察用法的反向延伸

zh-tw.content:
貼文：今天午餐很難吃。
留言區：留友看、留友看、留友看。

我滑下去，
看到第三十七個留友看。

所以朋朋們，
你們是要看什麼？
zh-tw.editorNote: 「留友看」是 2025-2026 Threads 演算法產物，留下這三字會把貼文推給朋友動態。但 2026 已經有人開始反向吐槽濫用——這個版本就是抓那種尷尬時刻，連雞毛蒜皮的事都被刷留友看
variations: 自嘲版：朋友限動只是早餐照，下面寫「留友看」，回他「友看完了，沒事。」

en.content:
Post: lunch was bad today.
Comments: save-for-friends, save-for-friends, save-for-friends.

I scroll down,
hit the 37th save-for-friends.

So, palsies,
what exactly are we looking at?
en.editorNote: 'Save-for-friends' (留友看) is a 2025-2026 Threads algorithm hack — three characters that push the post to your friends' feeds. But 2026 has its share of meta-complaints about overuse, and this entry catches that awkward moment when even the most trivial post gets flooded
variations: Self-roast version: friend's story is just a breakfast pic with 'save-for-friends' captioned — reply 'Friends saw it. Nothing happened.'

items.sourceNote: 「破防」原為遊戲術語突破防禦，2024-2026 延伸為心理防線被擊穿的萬用詞

zh-tw.content:
我以為我已經沒事了，
直到外送員看著我說：
「妳今天看起來好累喔。」

破防。
當場在便當前面哭。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「破防」原本是遊戲術語，2026 在 Threads 已經是萬用情緒詞，指那種「以為自己撐住了結果一句話被擊穿」的瞬間。重點是寫一個本來無關痛癢的小細節，最後三個字收尾
variations: 正向版：「看到他偷偷幫我留最後一塊雞排，破防。」用來形容被善意擊穿。

en.content:
I thought I was fine,
until the delivery guy looked at me and said,
'You look really tired today.'

Defenses down.
Sitting there crying over a lunch box.
en.editorNote: 'Defenses down' (破防) started as gaming jargon for breaking through armor. By 2026 it's the all-purpose Threads phrase for that moment when you think you're holding it together — and one tiny line cracks you open. Build up a mundane scene, then drop the two characters at the end
variations: Positive version: 'He saved the last piece of chicken for me without saying anything. Defenses down.' Use it for being floored by quiet kindness.

items.sourceNote: 「硬控」MOBA 遊戲術語，2025-2026 Threads 形容沉迷、停不下來的瞬間

zh-tw.content:
我只是想看一支三十秒的影片。

結果被硬控四十分鐘，
回神已經在看「貓咪如何認得自己的名字」第七集。

演算法你贏了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「硬控」原本是 MOBA 用語，指角色被定住沒辦法動。2026 用來形容被某段內容綁住、滑不掉。重點寫「本來只想看一下下」對比「結果整個被吸走」，越荒謬的主題越好笑
variations: 稱讚版：「她笑起來硬控我五秒。」用來形容被某人的某個瞬間電到。

en.content:
Just opening a 30-second clip.

Forty minutes later, still locked in,
now on episode 7 of 'How Cats Recognize Their Own Names.'

Algorithm, you win.
en.editorNote: 'Locked in' (硬控) is borrowed from MOBA games where a character literally can't move. In 2026 Taiwan slang it describes content that nails you to the screen. The trick: contrast 'I'll just check for a sec' with 'I am still here.' The dumber the rabbit hole, the better
variations: Compliment version: 'Her smile locked me down for five seconds.' Use it when someone's split-second moment short-circuits your brain.

items.sourceNote: 「觸爛」=「真的同意到爆」，2025-2026 Threads 強烈贊同語

zh-tw.content:
朋朋說：
「上班最累的不是工作，
是回完訊息還要表現得很有空。」

我：觸爛。
整個會議室的我都站起來鼓掌。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「觸」從英文 true 來，「觸爛」就是同意到爆的意思，2026 還在 Threads 高強度使用。寫一句很犀利的觀察，下面接「觸爛」兩個字，再用誇張畫面收尾，效果最好
variations: 短促版：朋友抱怨家庭聚會被問薪水，回「觸爛。」一句帶過所有委屈。

en.content:
Palsy says:
'The most exhausting part of work isn't the work —
it's replying to messages and pretending you've got time to spare.'

Me: Hard true.
Entire boardroom inside my head stands and applauds.
en.editorNote: 'Hard true' (觸爛) is a Threads-era amplifier — 'true' borrowed from English, intensified into 'true to the bone.' Use it to violently agree with something. Best formula: someone says a sharp observation, you reply with the two characters, then add an exaggerated visual
variations: Short version: friend complains about being grilled about salary at family dinner — just reply 'Hard true.' Two words, fully witnessed.

items.sourceNote: 「M3」=「You know what I'm saying」的諧音縮寫，2025-2026 句尾語

zh-tw.content:
我想要的不多，
就是月薪十萬、住信義區、
下班還有體力做副業。

不貪心，M3？
zh-tw.editorNote: 「M3」是英文 you know what I'm saying 唸快一點的諧音，2026 變成台灣 Threads 句尾的標配。用法很簡單：先寫一段超不合理的願望或想法，最後丟「M3？」假裝在徵求認同
variations: 閒聊版：「我覺得週四是禮拜的高潮，週五已經在收尾，M3？」用來開啟廢話討論。

en.content:
I'm not asking for much.
Just six-figure salary, a place downtown,
and enough energy after work for a side hustle.

Not greedy, nam-sayin?
en.editorNote: 'Nam-sayin' (M3) is the Taiwan Threads phonetic shortcut for 'you know what I'm saying' said fast. By 2026 it's the default sentence-ender for soft sarcasm. Formula: drop an absurdly ambitious wish, then tag 'nam-sayin?' to pretend you're seeking validation
variations: Filler version: 'Thursday is the peak of the week, Friday is already wrap-up mode, nam-sayin?' Great for kicking off a low-stakes debate.

items.sourceNote: 「很躁」2024-2026 持續流行的情緒詞，指坐立難安、煩到爆

zh-tw.content:
冷氣壞了。
外送遲到。
群組三百則未讀。

我沒有生氣，
我只是很躁、很躁、很躁。

躁到想把手機扔進冰箱。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「很躁」是 2024 後一直活在 Threads 的情緒詞，比「煩」更毛躁、比「生氣」更沒方向，是那種找不到對象只能整個人發毛的狀態。連寫三次「很躁」效果最好，最後丟一個荒謬畫面收尾
variations: 簡短版：朋友傳長文抱怨主管，回「我也很躁。」共感不必多話。

en.content:
AC broken.
Delivery late.
Group chat: 300 unread.

I'm not angry.
I'm just antsy, antsy, antsy.

Antsy enough to throw the phone in the freezer.
en.editorNote: 'Antsy' (很躁) has been alive on Taiwan Threads since 2024 — fizzier than 'annoyed,' less directed than 'angry.' It's the diffuse twitchy feeling with no real target. The move: repeat the word three times, then close on something physically ridiculous
variations: Short version: friend sends a long rant about their boss — reply 'Same, antsy too.' Solidarity doesn't need extra words.

items.sourceNote: 「emo」2025-2026 持續活躍的情緒詞，QuickSeek 列為 Z 世代核心情緒語

zh-tw.content:
我沒事，
只是有點 emo。

沒到崩潰，
就是看到夕陽會放空、
聽到副歌會嘆氣、
打開冰箱不知道要拿什麼。

那種 emo。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「emo」在 2026 已經完全脫離搖滾樂分類，變成 Threads 上最萬用的情緒擋箭牌。比「難過」輕，比「沒事」誠實，是那種說不出原因的小型陰天。要用得自然就是接「就是⋯」再列三個微小到很真實的細節
variations: 對話版：朋友問你怎麼了，回「emo 一下下而已，不用擔心。」既不關上門也不開太大。

en.content:
I'm fine,
just a little emo.

Not full breakdown,
just — staring at the sunset too long,
sighing at song choruses,
opening the fridge with no idea what I came for.

That kind of emo.
en.editorNote: By 2026 'emo' on Taiwan Threads has nothing to do with rock music — it's the all-purpose emotional shield. Lighter than 'sad,' more honest than 'fine.' The trick is to follow it with 'just —' and list three tiny details so specific they ring true
variations: Reply version: friend asks what's wrong — say 'A little emo, nothing huge.' Half open, half closed door.

items.sourceNote: 「脆」= Threads 中文暱稱（Threads→thread→脆餅→脆），2026 已是預設代稱

zh-tw.content:
今天在脆上看到一句話：
「成年人的崩潰是靜音的。」

我點了愛心，
滑下一則，
繼續靜音崩潰。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「脆」是 Threads 在台灣的中文外號（thread→脆餅→脆），2026 已經沒人會說「我在 Threads 上看到⋯⋯」全部都直接講「我在脆上看到⋯⋯」。用法很自然：講完看到的金句，再用一個自嘲動作把自己放進去
variations: 簡短版：「我脆滑到三點，明天又要崩潰。」一句話交代失眠 + 罪惡感。

en.content:
Saw a line on Cui today:
'Adult breakdowns are on mute.'

I tapped the heart,
scrolled to the next post,
kept breaking down on mute.
en.editorNote: 'Cui' (脆, literally 'crisp') is what Taiwanese users call Threads in 2026 — from thread → crisp wafer → crisp. By now no one says 'I saw on Threads,' it's always 'I saw on Cui.' The move: quote the sharp line you saw, then drop yourself back into the scroll with a quiet self-jab
variations: Short version: 'Scrolled Cui till 3am, gonna break down again tomorrow.' One sentence covers insomnia and guilt.

items.sourceNote: 「切爾西」源自抖音「英雄可以受委屈，但不能踩我的切爾西」，2026 仍在 Threads 流通

zh-tw.content:
你要笑我胖，可以。
你要嫌我廢，可以。
但你不能踩我的切爾西。

那是我最後一點尊嚴，
還是限量款。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「切爾西」原本是雙靴子，2026 已經變成「底線」的代名詞。意思是「其他什麼都可以開玩笑，唯獨這一條別碰」。用法公式：先連寫兩到三個可以接受的攻擊，最後用「但你不能踩我的切爾西」收尾，接一個荒謬到不行的小理由
variations: 情境版：媽媽碎念你十件事，第十一件碰到你最在意的興趣，回「踩到我切爾西了。」全家都會笑出來。

en.content:
Call me chubby, fine.
Call me useless, fine.
But don't step on my Chelseas.

Those are my last shred of dignity,
limited edition, by the way.
en.editorNote: 'Chelseas' (切爾西) — short for Chelsea boots — became Taiwan Threads shorthand for 'the one line you can't cross.' Comes from a viral short-video punchline. Formula: stack two or three insults you'd shrug off, then close with 'don't step on my Chelseas' plus a ridiculous tiny reason
variations: Family version: mom nags you on ten things, hits your one hobby on the eleventh — reply 'That's my Chelseas.' Whole table laughs.

items.sourceNote: 「要確欸」=「你確定嗎？」省略口語版，2024 流行至今，2026 Threads 常見回應

zh-tw.content:
朋朋：「我打算下班直接去報名馬拉松。」

我：「要確欸？」

朋朋：「⋯⋯不確欸。」

好，那我們去吃鹹酥雞。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「要確欸」是「你確定欸（嗎）？」的省略版，台語語感更重。2026 在 Threads 是萬用回應，用來在朋友要做奇怪決定時打一個輕輕的剎車。對方接「不確欸」就代表自己被勸退，整段對話像兩人版相聲
variations: 獨白版：「想剪短髮、想辭職、想搬家——但要確欸？」拿來自言自語也很可以。

en.content:
Palsy: 'I'm signing up for a marathon right after work.'

Me: 'You sure-ie?'

Palsy: '…not sure-ie.'

Cool. Let's go get fried chicken instead.
en.editorNote: 'Sure-ie' (要確欸) is the Taiwanese-tinged shrunken form of 'are you sure?' It's the universal Threads brake-tap when a friend's about to make a weird decision. The full bit is a two-line skit: them announcing, you saying 'sure-ie?', them folding with 'not sure-ie.'
variations: Inner-monologue version: 'I wanna chop my hair, quit my job, and move — but sure-ie?' Works on yourself too.

items.sourceNote: 「高級大人」配「初級大人」2026 春節爆紅，本條從「高級大人」反向角度切入

zh-tw.content:
我以為三十歲會自動變成高級大人。

結果只是擁有更貴的咖啡杯，
和更難解的稅單。

而我打開稅單的反應是：
打給我媽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「高級大人」是 2026 春節 Threads 大紅的配對詞，指能獨立處理家務、會應酬、會繳稅的成熟版本。常見的反向用法就是「以為自己是高級大人，結果還是會打給媽媽」——把預設打掉，落差越大越好笑
variations: 情境版：朋友問你會不會自己報稅，回「我是初級大人，旁邊那位才是高級大人。」自動指向你媽或室友。

en.content:
I thought thirty would auto-upgrade me into an advanced adult.

Turns out I just own a pricier coffee mug
and a tax form I can't decode.

My response to opening the tax form?
Call mom.
en.editorNote: 'Advanced adult' (高級大人) blew up alongside 'beginner adult' on Taiwan Threads over Lunar New Year 2026 — meaning the version of you who handles family duties, small talk, and taxes without flinching. The standard joke move is to claim it, then undercut yourself by calling your mom
variations: Reply version: friend asks if you do your own taxes — say 'I'm a beginner adult, that one over there is the advanced adult.' Point at your mom or roommate.
